content.full_text:
/

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 26, 1989

Ron Pembleton, Associate Director
c/o Fund for Public Interest Research
1129 State St. #10-B
Santa Barbara, CA 93101

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-626

Dear Mr. Pembleton:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform
Act.1

Your letter dated October 26, 1989, correctly summarizes my
advice. However, since receiving the letter I have spoken with
you on the telephone several times to request additional
information and have provided the following advice based on the
additional information.

In our initial conversation, I advised you that Pesticide
Watch Inc. would be the sponsoring organization for the committee
primarily formed to support the initiative. However, information
provided in your letter and in our recent conversations indicates
that Pesticide Watch is merely an arm of Cal PIRG, and that Cal
PIRG is the sponsoring organization. Since Pesticide Watch Inc.
is just in the formative stage, it would not be considered the
sponsoring entity for the measure committee which is also being
formed. Once Pesticide Watch Inc. is in operation, it may become
the sponsoring entity in place of Cal PIRG. If that happens, you
should file an amendment to the Statement of Organization (Form
410) reflecting the change.

You have asked if it is necessary to file the Statement of
Organization upon the incorporation of Pesticide Watch. You must
file the Statement of Organization within 10 days of the
committee's receipt of contributions totaling $1,000 or more. If
Pesticide Watch Inc. has already received $1,000 or more in
contributions, it should have filed the Statement of Organization
within 10 days of that date.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Ron Pembleton
Page two

     If the committee is primarily formed to support the
qualification of the initiative, the first campaign statement
(Form 420) would be due 21 days after any signatures are
submitted, or 21 days after the deadline for filing petitions,
whichever is earlier.  The period covered would be January 1, 1989
through 7 days prior to the due date of the statement.

     However, if the political committee is primarily formed to
support passage of the initiative after it has qualified for the
ballot, campaign statements must be filed according to when the
measure is being voted upon.  During 1989, the committee's filing
obligations consist of semi-annual statements.  During 1990, pre-
election statements as well as semi-annual statements will be
required.  The appropriate filing schedule will be contained in
the 1990 FPPC "Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure
Provisions of the Political Reform Act for Ballot Measure
Committees," which will be available early next year.

     The committee formed by Pesticide Watch Inc. will not also be
required to file statements as a "major donor committee."
However, it is possible that Cal PIRG may incur a filing
requirement as a major donor if it makes contributions totaling
$10,000 or more in a calendar year to candidates or political
committees.

     A copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience.

     If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        [illegible]

                                        By:  Wayne P. Imberi
                                             Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

FPPC
October 28, 1989
OCT 27 2:59 PM '09

Mr. Wayne Imberry
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J St.
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Wayne:

I am writing to follow up on matters that I spoke with you
regarding the establishment and reporting requirements of a "sponsored
recipient committee" CALPING is about to create. I wanted to make
sure I am clear on the details surrounding this action.

BACKGROUND

As you may recall from our conversation, for some months now
CALPING has been working on pesticide reform through an unincorporated
association called Pesticide Watch. One of the goals of Pesticide
Watch has been to pass effective state reform through either
legislative action, or if need be, by way of initiative. Frustrated
by inaction of the legislature, a major statewide initiative has been
announced on pesticides reform, as well as other matters. It is at
this point in time that we are interested in having Pesticide Watch
take a support position and become involved in the campaign.

PROPOSED STRUCTURE

It has been suggested that we should incorporate Pesticide Watch
as a 501 (c) 4 exempt organization, and have Pesticide Watch set up a
sponsored recipient committee (Pesticide Watch being the sponsor).

HOW WOULD THIS WORK

In order to ask the questions I have, a little more background
information is necessary:

Pesticide Watch Income:
* Currently California citizens are being asked to become members
of Pesticide Watch. The average membership contribution is
$25-40. Currently, over 2,000 citizens are members of the
association. These citizens understand that the goals of
Pesticide Watch are to pass effective reform through the most
appropriate forum (legislative or initiative)
* Core staff from Pesticide Watch will seek large individual
and/or group donations to support campaign efforts.
* Initial loans will be sought from CALPING to start up the
organization.

MR. WAYNE IMBERRY
Page Two
10/16/89

Campaign Committee Account:
* Most income from Pesticide Watch would be turned over to
the sponsored committee - mainly in large chunks of money
at a time. Campaign related expenditures such as salaries
and miscellaneous expenses would be paid for and tracked
out of this account.

REQUIRED FPPC REPORTING

1) It is my understanding that once Pesticide Watch incorporates, we
would then file a Statement of Organization Report (Form 410) to
establish the committee. My questions flow from this first report.

a) When is Pesticide Watch required to file? I saw conflicting
requirements in the FPPC Information Manual. Is it within 10 days of
receiving $1,000 which would be immediately upon placing money in the
account, or within 10 days of becoming a committee?

2) Semi-Annual Statement Reports (Form 420) are required to be
submitted by January 31, 1990 and June 31, 1990. These reports
disclose specific financial information pertaining to contributions
and expenditures. I have the following questions about these reports:

a) In speaking with you the other day, I was trying to clarify the
issue of tracking and reporting small contributions. Under the above
scenario, Pesticide Watch would be receiving thousands of
contributions in the range of $25-100. This money would then be
turned over in lump sums to the sponsored committee. If, for example,
Pesticide Watch deposited $10,000 in 1,000 member contributions of
$25 each, would we have to itemize each contribution in our list?
You suggested that we would only have to issue a roster of those
contributors of $100 or more. On the other hand, there is reference
in the manual to tracking each donor in the event they contribute
over $100 annually which would then be required to report. Would we
still be under this reporting requirement?

As you can see, the reason I raise these questions is due to
convenience in administering the campaign. The number of contributors
over $100 would be few relative to the thousands of smaller
contributors.

b) The other issue I am confused about is in reference to the status
of Pesticide Watch. If this organization is turning over large sums
of money, does it not qualify as a major donor committee? You seemed
to think not, but I wanted to double check.

c) The third point has to do with Pesticide Watch activity which has
already occurred. Substantial funds have been raised by the association
prior to the announcement of the initiative. With the anticipated
steps we will need to take, significant sums of money would be
immediately deposited in the campaign account. Would we have to issue

Mr. Wayne Imberry
Page Three
10/16/89

a detailed report on the funds that have already been contributed to
Pesticide Watch, such as contributors over $100 (and possibly tracking
each $25 or larger contribution - depending on your answer to (a)
above)?

PRE-ELECTION REPORTING

I am also unclear as to the number and timing of the pre-election
statements (form 495) we would be required to file. Would we have to
file a 1st and a 2nd pre-election statement?

Thanks for your assistance on this matter.

Sincerely,

Ron Pembleton
Associate Director
c/o Fund for Public Interest Research
1129 State St. #10-B
Santa Barbara, 93101

November 2, 1989


Ron Pembleton
Associate Director
c/o Fund for Pub. Interest Research
1129 State Street, #10-B
Santa Barbara, CA 93101

Re: Letter No. 89-626

Dear Mr. Pembleton:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on October 27, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,


Jeanne Pritchard
Chief Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: The requester sought confirmation and additional clarification regarding campaign disclosure filing requirements for a political committee formed to support a pesticide-related initiative, including questions about the sponsoring organization, filing timelines, and major donor reporting obligations.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Cal PIRG is the sponsoring organization (not Pesticide Watch Inc.), the committee must file a Statement of Organization within 10 days of receiving $1,000 or more in contributions, and filing obligations depend on whether the committee supports initiative qualification or passage, with semi-annual statements required in 1989 and additional pre-election statements in 1990.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of two parts: (1) November 2, 1989 acknowledgment letter from Jeanne Pritchard (Chief Technical Assistance Division), and (2) December 26, 1989 advice response from Kathryn Donovan (General Counsel). No traditional QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present; extracted question and conclusion synthetically from letter body. High confidence in extraction as the substantive advice is clearly presented in the December 26 letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requester sought confirmation and additional clarification regarding campaign disclosure filing requirements for a political committee formed to support a pesticide-related initiative, including questions about the sponsoring organization, filing timelines, and major donor reporting obligations.

CONCLUSION: Cal PIRG is the sponsoring organization (not Pesticide Watch Inc.), the committee must file a Statement of Organization within 10 days of receiving $1,000 or more in contributions, and filing obligations depend on whether the committee supports initiative qualification or passage, with semi-annual statements required in 1989 and additional pre-election statements in 1990.

embedding.summary: FPPC General Counsel Kathryn Donovan confirms and clarifies telephone advice provided to Ron Pembleton regarding campaign disclosure requirements for a political committee supporting an initiative. The response addresses sponsoring organization identification, Statement of Organization filing requirements, and campaign statement filing schedules based on the committee's purpose.
